title: Warm up
content: This slide introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through illustrations of taxi drivers experiencing each city from inside their cars. Each image reflects a distinct urban environment and technological context specific to that city.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with heavy traffic, skyscrapers, and traditional landmarks alongside modern navigation technology.
Chongqing is shown with a driver reading a book amid mountainous, layered architecture and a cable car.
Hangzhou features a driver using a smartphone app with AI/robot interface elements near a scenic lake.

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This is a title slide introducing the presentation's theme, 'A Ride Through China,' using imagery of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture from different cities. It sets up the metaphor of exploring China's development through a taxi ride perspective.
key_claims: The presentation is themed as 'A Ride Through China'.
Imagery includes a taxi sign and traditional/modern Chinese buildings representing different cities.

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure for Session 1, presenting a three-step process for investigating city development: identifying local features, developing strategies, and achieving outcomes with positive impact. An isometric cityscape illustration visually represents urban development.
key_claims: The investigation process follows three sequential steps: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact.
The session focuses on researching how cities develop based on their unique characteristics.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This slide presents a matching exercise linking three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—to their respective development strategies. Lines connect each city to a strategy, with supporting images of city landmarks on the right.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with 'Sustainable urban development'.
Chongqing is matched with 'Tourism & Economic growth'.
Hangzhou is matched with 'Technology & tradition integration (融合)'.

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide synthesizes the taxi driver perspective across three cities to illustrate that China's development is diverse and locally tailored. It reinforces the connection between each city's driving scene and its corresponding development strategy, emphasizing customized development approaches.
key_claims: Beijing's scene represents sustainable urban development.
Chongqing's scene represents tourism & economic growth.
Hangzhou's scene represents technology & tradition integration.
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜).
